sections.paragraph:
φϑάνω [etym. uncertain]

—1. ‘be first in arriving at a point’, be ahead of, precede 1 Th 4:15. In dramatic use of verbs relating to foot movement in Ro 9 (διώκω pursue, vs. 31; προσκόπτω stumble, vs. 32) φ. w. the neg. Ro 9:31 denotes failure to arrive first (i.e. ahead of the Gentiles, for, as vs. 32b points out, Israel ‘stumbled’); others place the usage under

—2. ‘reach a point’, arrive, reach w. diminished aspect of being ahead in doing someth.

—a. w. focus on a target group Mt 12:28; Lk 11:20; 2 Cor 10:14; 1 Th 2:16.

—b. w. focus on a state or condition (cp. Ro 9:31 in 1 above); Phil 3:16.
